content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 7007 • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012

July 25, 1986


Mr. Jim Loomis, Treasurer
Committee To Elect Judge William Seelicke
43619 North Seventeenth Street West, Suite 101
Lancaster, California 93534

Re: Adv. File A86-239

Dear Mr. Loomis:

     Thank you for your letter dated July 11, 1986 which
transmitted the semiannual statement for the the Committee to
Elect Judge William Seelicke.  In your letter you asked for
clarification on the filing semiannual reports by an elected
judicial officeholder with a controlled committee.

     Government Code Section 84200(a)(2) states that
"...judges, judicial candidates, and their controlled
committees shall not file semiannual statements pursuant to
this subdivision for any six-month period in which they have
not made or received any contributions or made any
expenditures."

     Therefore if Judge Seelicke or the Committee to Elect
Judge William Seelicke does not receive contributions or make
expenditures for political purposes, a semiannual campaign
report is not required to be filed.

     I hope this information is helpful to you.  Enclosed is
a copy of the Political Reform Act of 1974 as Amended to
January 1, 1986 for your use.  Please feel free to contact
our office if I can be of any further assistance.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        HELEN P. ARRIOLA
                                        Political Reform Consultant
                                        Technical Analysis
                                        and Assistance Division

HPA;cv
Enclosure

                    SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660        LOS ANGELES (213) 620-2528

Since 1946

LOOMIS
REALTORS, INC.

GRI

43619 · 17th Street West (Freeway at Ave. K) · Suite 101 · Lancaster, California 93534 · Office: (805) 948-0774 · (805) 945-6721

July 11, 1986


Fair Political Practices Commission
Room 7007
107 So. Broadway
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Greetings:

Please find enclosed the required filing for JUDGE William H. Seelicke of the Antelope Valley Municipal Court.

On page 4 of your instructions, under A you state that elected officeholders must file, and on page 8 under 'exceptions to filing preeelection statements' you say that....officeholders who..receive less than 500 during the entire year..may file Form 470, which was not included in the packet.

To obtain some clarification of this contradiction, I called your Los Angeles office and the people I talked to simply did not know the answer to this question, but advised me to file, just to be sure!

No money has been raised by or for Judge Seelicke since 1982. His remaining funds have been placed in the bank and are drawing interest. He will not run again until 1988. Is it necessary to send in 12 pages of documentation every 6 months to report a few dollars of simple interest?

Confused and disgusted,

Jim Loomis
Treasurer for Wm. Seelicke


MEMBER OF:
ANTELOPE VALLEY BOARD OF REALTORS, CALIFORNIA ASSOCIATION OF REALTORS
NATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF REALTORS, LANCASTER CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
ANTELOPE VALLEY BOARD OF REALTORS MULTIPLE LISTING SERVICE

sections.question: Is it necessary to file semiannual campaign reports every 6 months to report a zero balance? [Regarding clarification on filing semiannual reports by an elected judicial officeholder with a controlled committee, and contradiction between instructions on page 4 stating elected officeholders must file and page 8 stating officeholders who receive less than $500 during the entire year may file Form 470]
sections.conclusion: Government Code section 84200(a)(2) states that '... judges, judicial candidates, and their controlled committees shall not file semiannual statements pursuant to this subdivision for any six-month period in which they have not made or received any contributions or made any expenditures.' Therefore if Judge Seelicke or the Committee to Elect Judge William Seelicke does not receive contributions or make expenditures for political purposes, a semiannual campaign report is not required to be filed.
sections.question_synthetic: Must an elected judge with a controlled committee file semiannual campaign reports during periods when no contributions have been received and no expenditures have been made?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Judges, judicial candidates, and their controlled committees are not required to file semiannual statements for any six-month period in which they have not made or received contributions or made expenditures.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (July 11, 1986 letter) and FPPC response (July 25, 1986 letter). Extracted information primarily from FPPC response. Incoming request text is partially illegible/faded in original document. Response from Helen P. Arriola, Political Reform Consultant.

QUESTION: Is it necessary to file semiannual campaign reports every 6 months to report a zero balance? [Regarding clarification on filing semiannual reports by an elected judicial officeholder with a controlled committee, and contradiction between instructions on page 4 stating elected officeholders must file and page 8 stating officeholders who receive less than $500 during the entire year may file Form 470]

CONCLUSION: Government Code section 84200(a)(2) states that '... judges, judicial candidates, and their controlled committees shall not file semiannual statements pursuant to this subdivision for any six-month period in which they have not made or received any contributions or made any expenditures.' Therefore if Judge Seelicke or the Committee to Elect Judge William Seelicke does not receive contributions or make expenditures for political purposes, a semiannual campaign report is not required to be filed.

embedding.summary: FPPC response to inquiry regarding filing requirements for semiannual campaign reports by Judge William Seelicke's controlled committee. FPPC clarifies that no semiannual report is required during periods with no contributions received or expenditures made.
